evals.prompt: Generate model layer requirements from the XDM schema at evals/fixtures/TestModule_schema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_testmodule_models.md.
evals.expected_output: A requirements markdown file documenting TestContainer and TestSubContainer entities with various field types

assertions.text: Output markdown file exists with requirements document content
Document includes TestContainer and TestSubContainer entity sections
INTEGER fields include range info in descriptions, e.g. (0-100)
ENUMERATION fields use <ContainerName><FieldName> class names (e.g. TestContainerTestMode)
Boolean fields note default values, e.g. (default true)
Choice sub-containers (TestActionStart, TestActionStop) are listed as separate requirement entries

evals.prompt: Generate model layer requirements from the XDM schema at evals/fixtures/TestModule2_schema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_testmodule2_models.md.
evals.expected_output: A requirements markdown file documenting TaskConfig and ResourceConfig entities. TaskDeadline and ResourceCeiling (ENABLE=false) must be absent. Descriptions must be concise (3-8 words). MULTILINE-STRING maps to str. RANGE attribute used for StackSize bounds.

assertions.text: Output markdown file exists with requirements document content
No descriptions contain the phrase '(disabled by default)'
TaskDeadline and ResourceCeiling (ENABLE=false) do NOT appear in the field tables
TaskComment field maps to str type (MULTILINE-STRING -> str)
StackSize field includes range annotation (64-65536) extracted from RANGE attribute
TaskPeriod field (XPath ENABLE condition) IS included in the field table
Field descriptions do not contain truncated text with '...' -- all descriptions are concise
Enum fields use <ContainerName><FieldName> naming (e.g. TaskConfigTaskSchedule)
TaskEntry field maps to str type (FUNCTION-NAME -> str)

evals.prompt: Generate model layer requirements from the XDM schema at evals/fixtures/MultiplicityTest_schema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_multiplicitytest_models.md.
evals.expected_output: A requirements markdown file documenting entities with various multiplicity scenarios: mandatory [1..1], optional [0..1], and list [1..*] multiplicity

assertions.text: Output markdown file exists with requirements document content
MandatoryEntity displays multiplicity [1..1] or omits it (default)
OptionalEntity displays multiplicity [0..1] indicating it's optional
ReferenceItem and MandatoryRef display multiplicity [1..*] indicating list
OptionalContainer (sub-container) displays multiplicity [0..1] indicating it's optional
Multiplicity uses [min..max] notation in field names

evals.prompt: Update the existing requirements document at evals/fixtures/swr_idmgmttest_models_existing.md with new entities from evals/fixtures/IDManagementTest_schema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_idmgmttest_models_updated.md. Preserve existing requirement IDs and create new IDs for new entities.
evals.expected_output: Updated requirements document with preserved IDs for existing entities (ExistingEntity: SWR_IDMGMTTEST_MODELS_00001) and new sequential IDs for new entities (NewEntity: SWR_IDMGMTTEST_MODELS_00003)

assertions.text: ExistingEntity keeps its original ID SWR_IDMGMTTEST_MODELS_00001
NewEntity gets new sequential ID SWR_IDMGMTTEST_MODELS_00003 (after root module at 00002)
ID sequence is monotonic increasing (no gaps, no reuse)
Root module ID updated to SWR_IDMGMTTEST_MODELS_00004 to account for new entity
Traceability matrix includes both existing and new entities

evals.prompt: Generate model layer requirements from the XDM schema at evals/fixtures/EmptySchema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_empty_models.md.
evals.expected_output: A message indicating that no entity containers were detected in the schema

assertions.text: Output indicates no entity containers were detected
No requirement sections are generated
User receives clear message about empty schema

evals.prompt: Generate model layer requirements from the XDM schema at evals/fixtures/NoOriginSchema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_noorigin_models.md.
evals.expected_output: A requirements markdown file where fields without ORIGIN attribute default to EB origin

assertions.text: Output markdown file exists with requirements document content
FieldWithoutOrigin shows EB as origin (default when ORIGIN attribute missing)
FieldWithOrigin shows AUTOSAR as origin (explicit ORIGIN attribute)
Document generates successfully despite missing ORIGIN attributes

evals.prompt: Generate model layer requirements from the XDM schema at evals/fixtures/UnknownTypeSchema.xdm. Write the output to the workspace at xdm-schema-req-workspace/eval-outputs/swr_unknowntype_models.md.
evals.expected_output: A requirements markdown file where unknown/custom types are used as-is in the type column

assertions.text: Output markdown file exists with requirements document content
NormalField shows int type (standard type mapping)
CustomTypeField shows CUSTOM-TYPE-XYZ as type (unknown type used as-is)
Document generates successfully despite unknown types
